at_a_glance: You have spent $2.38 across 3 sessions, all on the same day. Session 2 was the expensive one — $1.30 covering all 9 Phase 1 foundation tasks plus the review pass. Session 3 dropped sharply to $0.47 because it was planning-only (F-STATE feature kit + spec review, no code written). Costs are healthy for where the project is; the expensive Phase 2 code sessions are still ahead.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: The project kickoff — brainstorm, architect, vibe-new-app, and spec review. Three planning tools in sequence, each reading all prior docs. $0.61 for the full spec and architecture foundation is reasonable; this context pays forward into every future session.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: The heaviest session — 9 Phase 1 foundation tasks (package.json through CODEBASE.md smoke test) plus the phase review pass and inline P1 fixes. At $1.30 and $0.12/task average this is in line with S-sized Electron scaffold work. The phase review and its parallel subagent orchestration added ~$0.35.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: A planning-only session — F-STATE feature kit creation (FEATURE_SPEC.md, FEATURE_PLAN.md, FEATURE_TASKS.md) plus spec review with 2 P1 fixes. No code written. At $0.47 this is the cheapest session so far and typical for a feature planning pass.

recommendations.title: Prompt caching not active after 3 sessions
recommendations.body: All three sessions show 0 cache_read_tokens. The active feature section in CLAUDE.md is updated each feature cycle, which resets caching. Splitting CLAUDE.md into a stable upper block and a dynamic active-feature block would let the stable portion cache across turns. At 0% vs a realistic 60% cache hit rate, the input cost difference on a large Phase 2 session could be $0.20-0.40.

forecast_narrative: Phase 2 has 5 code features remaining (F-STATE build, F-FIRST-RUN, F-SPEECH, F-CLAUDE, F-ACTIONS) plus a review gate — estimate $3.70-5.20 to complete. Phase 3 polish (4 tasks + final review) is typically cheaper than the feature build phase — estimate $0.70-0.90. Total project spend is tracking toward $6-8 at current rates. Fixing caching before Phase 2 code sessions start could save $0.50-1.00 off the remaining estimate.
